title: Executable C-016 guard - ssotdeps cross-check verifies data+schema forbidden-paths symmetry

evidence.ref: Post-D031 audit (~1 day later) found 3 IR domain files (ir/domain/work-item.ir.json + work-item-created + list-work-items) had schemas covered via ir/schema/** glob but data files NOT in dumb-agent forbidden_paths. C-016 is prose only — nothing automated checked the symmetry, so the gap was silent. Exactly the failure mode C-016 was meant to prevent for future SSOT additions
031-forbid-ssot-schemas - established C-016 in prose; D032 makes it executable
022-ssotdeps-cross-check - introduced the cross-check subcommand; D032 extends it with a second cross-document rule
025-doc-freshness-guard - same executable-pattern as docfresh: turn a recurring SSOT-mirror failure mode into a CI gate so the round-trip cost drops to 0

guards.ref: internal/ssotdeps.VerifyForbiddenSymmetry — iterates ssot_artifacts with non-empty schema, asserts both path and schema are covered by dumb-agent forbidden_paths globs (reuses internal/agentguard.MatchAny). Wired into ssotdeps cross-check CLI which already runs in contracts.yml.
internal/agentguard.MatchAny - exported (was lowercase matchAny); the function ssotdeps reuses to glob-match paths against patterns
agent-roles.riido.json - dumb-agent forbidden_paths now includes ir/domain/** (paired with the existing ir/schema/**) so the IR pair is symmetrically covered
concept-map C-016 - rule updated to describe both the prose rule and its executable enforcement

examples: Pre-D032: simulated removal of ir/domain/** from dumb-agent forbidden_paths -> ssotdeps cross-check reports 3 C-016 asymmetry violations (one per IR doc).
Post-D032 with ir/domain/** in place: ssotdeps cross-check passes with 'OK (1 pending entries clean against concept-map; 7 (data,schema) pairs symmetric against dumb-agent forbidden_paths)'.
Future SSOT addition path: when adding a new ssot_artifact with schema, the dep map declares it AND agent-roles.riido.json must register both halves; otherwise contracts CI fails before merge.
counterexamples: Both halves uncovered is acceptable: C-016 + D032 only fire when one half is in forbidden_paths and the other isn't. An SSOT with no expected dumb-agent interaction can stay unprotected (designer review is the only safety net) without tripping the rule.
ssot_artifacts whose schema field is null or empty string are out of scope (no schema to pair with).
The check uses agentguard.MatchAny which handles ** and * globs the same way the runtime check does — no divergence between 'what the guard sees' and 'what agentguard actually enforces at PR time'.
